protein_name: T-cell surface glycoprotein CD3 epsilon chain

sae_features.label: N-terminal Sec-dependent signal peptide
sae_features.description: N-terminal Sec-dependent signal peptide, specifically the hydrophobic helical h‑region (and adjacent c‑region near the cleavage site) of secreted/single-pass cell-surface proteins, notably immunoglobulin-superfamily receptors and IgCAMs

sae_features.label: Immunoglobulin-like ectodomains
sae_features.description: Extracellular/luminal immunoglobulin-like beta-sandwich modules (IgSF V-, C1- and C2-set) and immediately adjacent inter-domain linkers in secretory and cell-surface proteins; the feature captures the Ig-like ectodomain scaffold used for adhesion and immune recognition, with weaker spillover to related FN3 beta-sandwich repeats.

sae_features.label: Ig-like extracellular beta-sandwich
sae_features.description: Extracellular beta-strand/loop-rich beta-sandwich domains predominantly from IgSF (V-, C1-, C2-set) ectodomains—capturing the structural scaffold used by immune/checkpoint receptors/ligands and cell-adhesion molecules, including viral envelope proteins that mimic these surfaces, not strictly limited to canonical Ig-set folds.

sae_features.label: IgSF extracellular beta-sandwich signature
sae_features.description: Structural signature of extracellular immunoglobulin-like (IgSF) domains—V-set/C1/C2 sets—highlighting the beta-sandwich core, with strongest emphasis on the C-terminal half of each domain (F/G strand region) adjacent to the conserved intradomain disulfide; broadly used in immune recognition and cell-adhesion receptors

sae_features.label: Disordered immunoreceptor cytoplasmic tails
sae_features.description: Intrinsically disordered cytoplasmic signaling tails of single-pass cell‑surface receptors—predominantly immune IgSF/NK/adhesion receptors—characterized by low‑complexity, S/T/Y- and proline‑rich sequences that frequently harbor tyrosine‑based immunoreceptor motifs (e.g., ITIM/ITSM/YxxL/I) and adapter‑binding segments

sae_features.label: Type I cytosolic tails
sae_features.description: Cytosolic C-terminal tails of single-pass type I membrane (and viral envelope) proteins, especially the juxtamembrane, intrinsically disordered segments enriched in acidic/Ser/Thr/Pro residues and tyrosine-based endocytic/signaling motifs (NPxY/YENPTY), which mediate adaptor binding, phosphorylation, and trafficking.

sae_features.label: Juxtamembrane ectodomain stalk
sae_features.description: Extracellular juxtamembrane “stalk/ectodomain stem” immediately N‑terminal to a transmembrane helix in metazoan cell‑surface glycoproteins (and analogous luminal loops before TMs in multipass proteins). These segments are typically Pro/Ser/Thr/Gly- and acidic‑rich, often low‑complexity or flexible with GP/PG clusters but may include short amphipathic helices; they frequently harbor N-/O‑glycosylation and protease shedding/processing sites. When an EGF‑ or SEA‑like module abuts the membrane, the feature extends into the C‑terminal part of that module.

sae_features.label: Ig-like YxC disulfide signature
sae_features.description: Conserved Ig-like domain signature around the intradomain disulfide: the Y–x–C (often Y–T–C) motif and adjacent loop/β-strand region (F-strand/FG loop in Ig numbering) in extracellular V-set and C2-set immunoglobulin folds across IgSF receptors, ligands, and viral Ig mimics
